steps.title: Identify what the answer must include
steps.content: The student’s goal is to emphasize the order in which the two winners received the A.M. Turing Award. A correct answer needs both winners named with their award years and explicit temporal language showing who came first.

steps.title: Eliminate wrong choices, confirm the correct one
steps.content:
• Choice A — ‘Butler W. Lampson won the A.M. Turing Award in 1992; Judea Pearl won it later in 2011.’: names both winners with years and uses “later” to mark Pearl’s award as coming after Lampson’s — sequence is explicit. ✓
  • Choice B — ‘It was in 1992 that Butler W. Lampson won the A.M. Turing Award.’: mentions only Lampson; Pearl is omitted entirely. ✗
  • Choice C — ‘Judea Pearl and Butler W. Lampson both won the A.M. Turing Award, which is given for “major contributions of lasting importance to computing.”’: names both winners but includes no years and no temporal marker showing sequence. ✗
  • Choice D — ‘Judea Pearl won the A.M. Turing Award in 2011, while Butler W. Lampson won it in 1992.’: gives both winners and years but “while” implies simultaneity rather than sequence. ✗
That matches choice A — A is correct.
